description: When the product reaches its end of life in the current building, product can be carefully deconstructed and refurbished locally or central inorder to be used in a new building project. This scenario has the highest potential for circularity, significantly reducing the need for new   raw materials in future productions of LIGNA.

When the product reaches its end of life in the current building, the wooden based floor panels are sent to an incineration facility, which is a CO2 intensive process. No materials are being recovered .

When the product reaches its end of life in the current building, the whole floor panels are being sent to a landfill facility, they stay on the dumping site for 100 years. Eventhough it has low CO2 values, scenario is not considered resource recovery friendly.

When the product reaches its end of life in the current building, the floor panel will be centrally shredded, The resulting gypsum granulate is used in a future panel production. This Scenario significantly reduce the need for new raw materials, like gypsum.

This scenario has the highest potential for circularity, significantly reducing the need for new   raw materials in future productions of Calcium Sulphate Panels.
